meta_title: For Founders: debugging with AI to Solve no portfolio (Intermediate Idea List)
meta_description: Learn how to debugging with AI and overcome no portfolio. A Intermediate-level Idea List using Antigravity and Claude Code.

content.seo_title: For Founders: Debugging with AI to Solve No Portfolio
content.seo_description: Overcome the 'no portfolio' hurdle by mastering AI-driven debugging. Learn to use Antigravity and Claude Code to build professional-grade SaaS products quickly.
content.answer_block: To overcome a lack of portfolio, build a high-impact debugging tool that demonstrates technical depth. Use Antigravity to structure your architecture and Claude Code to automate complex troubleshooting cycles. By solving real-world technical debt for others, you transform your lack of history into a showcase of high-velocity, production-ready engineering skills.
content.intro:
The blank screen of a portfolio page is the silent killer of founder ambition. You have the vision, but without a track record, potential users and investors see only a risk. The industry doesn't care about your resume; it cares about your ability to ship, iterate, and solve critical technical bottlenecks under pressure.

We are shifting the paradigm. Instead of building vanity projects, you will build a sophisticated debugging utility that proves your mastery of modern AI-assisted development. By leveraging Antigravity and Claude Code, you will create a high-value asset that demonstrates you can diagnose and resolve complex system failures, turning your 'no portfolio' status into a competitive advantage.

steps.title: Architecting the Diagnostic Engine
steps.description: Define the scope of your debugging utility. Focus on a specific niche, such as API latency analysis or database query optimization, to ensure your portfolio piece solves a high-value problem.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Create a robust, modular architecture for a CLI-based diagnostic tool that scans Node.js projects for memory leaks and performance bottlenecks.

steps.title: Implementing Intelligent Log Parsing
steps.description: Build the core logic that parses raw error logs into actionable insights. This demonstrates your ability to handle unstructured data and turn it into business intelligence.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Write a high-performance log parser that categorizes errors by severity and suggests specific code-level fixes using heuristic analysis.

steps.title: Automating Root Cause Analysis
steps.description: Integrate AI to suggest code patches. This is the 'wow' factor that shows you understand how to leverage LLMs for autonomous system repair.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Develop a function that compares identified error patterns against a known library of common anti-patterns to provide automated repair suggestions.

steps.title: Building the CLI Interface
steps.description: Ensure your tool is developer-friendly. A clean CLI interface is a hallmark of professional engineering and increases the perceived value of your portfolio piece.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Design a clean, interactive CLI interface using Commander.js that allows users to toggle between deep-scan and quick-check modes.

steps.title: Validating with Synthetic Data
steps.description: Stress-test your tool against complex edge cases. This proves your solution is production-ready, not just a proof of concept.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Generate a suite of synthetic error scenarios that simulate common production failures, including race conditions and database connection timeouts.

pro_tips: Always include a 'Why this matters' section in your README to frame your tool as a business solution rather than a coding exercise.
Use Claude Code to document your decision-making process in the commit history; this shows senior engineering maturity.
Focus on the 'Mean Time to Repair' (MTTR) metric in your tool's dashboard to prove direct ROI to potential clients.
Create a 'demo mode' that allows users to run your tool against a sample repository without needing their own credentials.
Leverage Antigravity to generate high-quality integration tests that prove your tool won't break existing production pipelines.
content.next_steps: Deploy your tool to GitHub, write a 'Case Study' blog post explaining the technical challenges you solved during development, and share it on IndieHackers to start gaining traction.
content.conversion_block: You are now equipped to build a portfolio that commands respect and showcases genuine engineering utility. Don't let the 'no portfolio' label hold you back; start building solutions that matter. Ready to build? Join our AI Builder community → Start your first project today.
